does: This paper makes the case that superintelligence governance deserves serious consideration and timely preparation, examining what governance structures and approaches might be necessary for AI systems that would far exceed the capabilities of artificial general intelligence. The work articulates why superintelligence presents distinctive governance challenges beyond those of current or near-term AI systems, and begins outlining the problem space and key considerations that would shape effective oversight mechanisms.
connects: This work builds on emerging literature addressing AI governance, safety, and alignment—extending beyond earlier narrow focuses on specific AI risks or near-term system control to confront the governance implications of more distant but potentially transformative scenarios. It responds to and complements prior research on existential risk from advanced AI, AI safety research on alignment and control, and governance frameworks developed for other emerging technologies, synthesizing these threads into a structured examination of long-term governance challenges.
enables: This paper opens space for serious interdisciplinary work on superintelligence governance institutions, potentially spurring research into governance architectures, international coordination mechanisms, corporate structures, and regulatory frameworks that could handle superintelligent systems. It may catalyze further investigation into how existing governance precedents from other domains could inform superintelligence oversight, and encourages policymakers, technologists, and researchers to begin developing conceptual and institutional foundations needed before such systems approach reality.
